_doc: Ground-truth labels for the precision/recall harness. 'expect' lists the ERROR/WARN rule_ids a domain reviewer judges SHOULD fire on each doc. INFO findings are advisory and are not scored as FP/FN (reported separately). Labels are independent of the code's design: where the tool fires an ERROR/WARN not in 'expect', that is a candidate false positive; where an expected rule does not fire, that is a candidate false negative. Profile used: mildoc.

good_cui_opord.md.note: Well-formed CUI OPORD: CUI banners top+bottom, full DI block, all 5 O-SMEAC sections, complete mission/execution. Advisory INFO ok (portion_marking_recommended, orientation_absent).

good_naval_letter.md.note: From/To/Subj in order; Subj uppercase, no terminal punctuation.

good_namp_record.md.note: Complete NAMP/CSEC record with parseable date. Advisory INFO ok (record_shape_ok).

good_warnord.md.note: WARNORD with mission+execution+timeline. Advisory INFO ok (orientation_absent).

plain_memo.md.note: Non-military prose. Noise test: nothing should fire at all.

bad_cui_banner.md.note: Invalid CUI//CTI banner, no DI block, exposed SSN, in CUI context.

bad_namp_missing.md.note: NAMP record missing corrective action, due date, evidence.

bad_opord_missing_cs.md.note: OPORD missing Command and Signal; weak mission and execution.

bad_fragord.md.note: FRAGORD with no base-order reference and omitted sections.

legacy_marking_doc.md.note: FOUO legacy marking + ITAR language without CUI marking.

empty.md.note: Empty document: must not crash, must not fire.

ssn_lookalike.md.note: SSN-shaped stock number in a supply doc. Domain-clean. Probes pii.ssn precision (high-recall regex is expected to over-fire here).
